strengths: Single shared codebase powers three delivery channels (extension, desktop, web) with minimal branching — platform.js adapter pattern is clean and effective
Provider abstraction (callProvider → 5 implementations) allows adding new LLM providers without touching UI code
Zero-backend design — all user data stays client-side; web mode only injects API keys server-side via edge function
Dual persistence layer (IndexedDB primary, localStorage fallback) handles browser limitations gracefully
Comprehensive error taxonomy with classified categories, user-facing messages, recovery suggestions, and actionable UI buttons
PII engine is well-isolated, configurable per-pattern, and includes Luhn validation — production-grade for a client-side tool
Hooks-based state management avoids Redux/Context boilerplate while keeping concerns separated (editor, library, UI, AB test, test cases)
Prompt schema normalization handles version migration and metadata evolution without breaking existing saved data
Well-documented — ARCHITECTURE.md and IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md explain design decisions and runtime model
weaknesses: App.jsx (972 LOC) is a monolithic orchestrator — all tab switching, keyboard shortcuts, state wiring, and layout logic in one file
PadTab.jsx (22K) is the largest component and handles both the editor surface and the library sidebar — two distinct concerns
No React Context or state container — hooks are passed as props through tab components, creating prop-drilling chains 3-4 levels deep
Test coverage is thin — 9 unit test files cover utilities and hooks, but no integration tests for the enhance flow or provider round-trips
Web demo mode couples frontend to Vercel deployment (proxy.js key injection) — no abstraction for alternative hosting
No feature flag system — capabilities like ghost variables, version history, and demo mode are hardcoded conditionals
Inline styles mixed with Tailwind classes in several components — inconsistent styling approach

design_issues.issue: App.jsx (972 LOC) orchestrates all state hooks, tab switching, keyboard shortcuts, modals, toast notifications, and layout — too many responsibilities for a single component
design_issues.impact: Every feature addition touches App.jsx, increasing merge conflicts and cognitive load. Difficult to test in isolation.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Extract feature shells: EditorShell (wires usePromptEditor + PadTab), LibraryShell (wires usePromptLibrary), ABTestShell. App.jsx becomes a thin layout + router with <TabRouter> dispatching to shells. Each shell owns its hook wiring.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Medium (2-3 sessions) — mostly moving code, no logic changes

design_issues.issue: PadTab.jsx (22K) renders both the prompt editor surface AND the library sidebar with search/filter/drag-reorder — two distinct UX domains in one file
design_issues.impact: Changes to library UX risk breaking editor rendering. File is too large to review in one pass.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Split into PadEditor.jsx (editor textarea, enhance button, diff view, lint panel) and LibrarySidebar.jsx (search, filters, card list, drag-reorder). PadTab becomes a layout wrapper: <PadTab><LibrarySidebar /><PadEditor /></PadTab>
design_issues.effort_estimate: Medium (1-2 sessions) — file split, no logic changes

design_issues.issue: State from hooks in App.jsx is passed 3-4 levels deep through tab components. Example: usePromptEditor returns ~20 values, all threaded through PadTab props.
design_issues.impact: Adding new state requires updating prop signatures through the entire chain. Components receive props they only forward.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Introduce a lightweight PromptLabContext (or two: EditorContext + LibraryContext) at the App level. Tab components useContext() instead of accepting prop bundles. Keep hooks as-is — Context just replaces the prop-drilling bridge.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Small-Medium (1 session) — mechanical replacement of props with useContext()

design_issues.issue: No provider interface contract — each provider function (callAnthropic, callOpenAI, etc.) has a similar but subtly different signature. No TypeScript interface or JSDoc contract enforces consistency.
design_issues.impact: Adding a new provider requires reading existing implementations to discover the expected shape. Easy to miss edge cases (streaming, error format, auth header).
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Define a ProviderAdapter interface (JSDoc or .d.ts): { name, call(payload, settings, fetchImpl), formatMessages(payload), parseResponse(raw), validateSettings(settings) }. Each provider exports an object implementing this interface. callProvider() dispatches by looking up the adapter.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Small (1 session) — restructure existing functions into adapter objects

design_issues.issue: Capabilities like ghost variables, version history, demo mode, and web mode are controlled by scattered conditionals (import.meta.env.VITE_WEB_MODE, platform detection, hardcoded booleans) with no central registry
design_issues.impact: No way to toggle features without code changes. Hard to reason about what's enabled in which platform/build.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Create lib/features.js exporting a frozen flags object derived from build-time env vars and runtime detection: { webMode, demoMode, ghostVariables, versionHistory, piiScanning }. Components check features.ghostVariables instead of inline conditionals.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Small (< 1 session) — extract existing checks into one module

design_issues.issue: 9 unit test files cover utilities and hooks, but no integration tests for the core enhance flow (user input → provider call → response display) or cross-platform behavior. E2E is a single smoke test.
design_issues.impact: Regressions in the critical path (enhance, save, load) are caught manually. Provider format changes can break silently.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Add integration tests for: (1) enhance flow with mocked provider responses, (2) library save/load round-trip, (3) A/B test experiment persistence, (4) PII scan → redact → send flow. Use MSW (Mock Service Worker) for provider API mocking.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Medium (2-3 sessions) — test infrastructure + 4-5 integration tests

design_issues.issue: Three separate persistence mechanisms (localStorage via storage.js, IndexedDB via experimentStore.js, chrome.storage.local in background.js) with no unified interface. Each has its own error handling and serialization.
design_issues.impact: Data migration across platforms requires touching three codepaths. No single place to implement encryption-at-rest or export/import.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Create lib/dataStore.js with a unified interface: { get(collection, key), put(collection, key, value), list(collection, query), delete(collection, key) }. Implementations: IndexedDBStore (primary), LocalStorageStore (fallback), ChromeStorageStore (extension settings). experimentStore and storage.js become thin wrappers.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Medium (2 sessions) — new abstraction layer + migration of existing callers

design_issues.issue: Components mix Tailwind utility classes, inline style objects, CSS custom properties (constants.js tagColors), and raw className strings. No single styling convention.
design_issues.impact: Difficult to maintain design consistency. Theme changes require updating multiple styling mechanisms.
design_issues.refactoring_strategy: Standardize on Tailwind for all component styling. Convert inline styles to Tailwind classes or @apply directives. Move tagColors from JS constants to Tailwind theme extensions. This is cosmetic — defer until a design system pass.
design_issues.effort_estimate: Large (3+ sessions) — touches every component, high diff volume

structural_improvements: Extract App.jsx into thin layout shell + feature shells (EditorShell, LibraryShell, ABTestShell) to reduce the 972-LOC monolith to ~200 LOC
Split PadTab.jsx (22K) into PadEditor + LibrarySidebar — two files, one layout wrapper
Introduce EditorContext and LibraryContext to eliminate 3-4 level prop drilling chains
Define a formal ProviderAdapter interface (JSDoc or .d.ts) so new providers can be added by implementing a known contract
Create lib/features.js to centralize feature flag checks currently scattered as inline conditionals
Add 4-5 integration tests covering the enhance flow, library round-trip, A/B persistence, and PII redaction pipeline
Unify storage behind a single DataStore interface with IndexedDB/localStorage/chrome.storage implementations
Add JSDoc @typedef blocks to hooks return values — usePromptEditor returns ~20 values with no type documentation

recommended_patterns.use_case: Provider implementations — replace switch/case dispatch with registered adapter objects
recommended_patterns.example: const geminiAdapter = { name: 'gemini', call(payload, settings, fetchImpl) { ... }, formatMessages(payload) { return toGeminiContents(payload); }, parseResponse(raw) { ... } }; registry.register(geminiAdapter);

recommended_patterns.use_case: Replace prop drilling from App.jsx through tab components — EditorContext provides hook values to any descendant
recommended_patterns.example: const EditorContext = createContext(); function EditorProvider({ children }) { const editor = usePromptEditor(); return <EditorContext.Provider value={editor}>{children}</EditorContext.Provider>; }

recommended_patterns.use_case: Centralize platform/build-dependent feature checks into one queryable object
recommended_patterns.example: export const features = Object.freeze({ webMode: import.meta.env.VITE_WEB_MODE === 'true', demoMode: Boolean(import.meta.env.VITE_DEMO_KEY), ghostVars: true, piiScanning: true });

recommended_patterns.use_case: Unify localStorage, IndexedDB, and chrome.storage behind one interface for library, experiments, and settings
recommended_patterns.example: class PromptRepository { constructor(store) { this.store = store; } async save(entry) { await this.store.put('prompts', entry.id, entry); } async find(id) { return this.store.get('prompts', id); } async list(query) { return this.store.list('prompts', query); } }

recommended_patterns.use_case: Replace the monolithic App.jsx tab switch with self-contained tab modules that register themselves
recommended_patterns.example: <TabRouter default='pad'><TabRouter.Tab id='pad' label='Pad'><EditorShell /></TabRouter.Tab><TabRouter.Tab id='ab' label='A/B Test'><ABTestShell /></TabRouter.Tab></TabRouter>

migration_path: Phase 1 — Reduce App.jsx (1 session): Extract EditorContext + LibraryContext, move hook wiring out of App.jsx into provider components. App.jsx drops to ~200 LOC layout + tab routing.
Phase 2 — Split PadTab (1 session): Extract LibrarySidebar.jsx and PadEditor.jsx from PadTab.jsx. PadTab becomes a flex layout wrapper.

Phase 3 — Formalize provider interface (1 session): Define ProviderAdapter shape, refactor callAnthropic/callOpenAI/callGemini/callOpenRouter/callOllama into adapter objects. Add providerRegistry.register().
Phase 4 — Feature flags (< 1 session): Create lib/features.js, replace scattered conditionals. No behavior change.
Phase 5 — Integration tests (2 sessions): Add MSW for provider mocking. Write tests for enhance flow, library CRUD, A/B persistence, PII redaction.
Phase 6 — Unified storage (2 sessions): Build DataStore interface with IndexedDB + localStorage + chrome.storage backends. Migrate experimentStore and storage.js callers.
Phase 7 — Styling pass (defer): Standardize on Tailwind, remove inline styles. Do this when a design system or rebrand is planned.
